metadata.notes: Content overhaul to Wingspan standard — Rogue B, March 2026. Corrected per official rulebook, BGG FAQ, UltraBoardGames rules reference.

key_rulings.text: On your turn, place your Action token on one section of your Player Mat. You cannot repeat the same action you took on your previous turn.
A Seeker without resources may move up to 2 spaces. A Seeker carrying resources may move only 1 space.
If the attacker and defender have equal totals, the attacker wins.
If your Friendship is below 3, you cannot earn any trophies until you restore it to 3 or higher.
Once a trophy is earned, it cannot be lost, even if the condition that earned it is no longer met.
You may earn at most 1 trophy per turn, except during the Grand Finale.
To earn the Apple Delivery trophy, deliver exactly 4 apples to Castle Everfree in a single turn. Same for gems.
When you Seek, placing a resource on a space occupied by an opponent's Seeker grants you +1 Friendship per resource placed there.
Bake Pies: remove 2 apples, gain 2 pies. Conjure Spell: remove 2 gems, draw 1 spell. Power Up: remove 1 apple + 1 gem, draft a power-up tile.
The Pie Fight Victory trophy can be earned by winning as attacker OR defender. It is the only trophy that can be earned on another player's turn.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Board & Tiles
- **1 game board** (double-sided) — This is a hex-grid map of the Kingdom of Pomme. You'll see Castle Everfree sitting right at the center, surrounded by territories, with base camp spaces around the outer edge. Notice the pink-bordered spaces — those are **portals**, and they connect to each other across the board (you'll use these as shortcuts during movement).
- **7 base camp tiles** — One for each animal kingdom. Your base camp marks where your seekers begin the game and where they retreat to if they lose a pie fight.
- **16 power-up tiles** (8 Move, 8 Make) — These are double-sided tiles you'll draft during the Make action. Each one permanently upgrades one of your actions for the rest of the game.

#### Miniatures & Tokens
- **14 seeker miniatures** — 2 per animal kingdom (7 kingdoms in the base game). Your pair of seekers shares a color, and they're your adventurers on the board.
- **28 trophy tokens** (4 per player) — You'll place these on the Trophy Track as you earn trophies throughout the game.
- **7 friendship tokens** — One per player, tracked on the Friendship Track (ranges from 0 to 8, and you start at 3).
- **7 pie tokens** — One per player, tracked on the Pie Track (ranges from 0 to 10, and you start at 3).
- **7 action tokens** — You'll place yours on your Player Mat each turn to show which action you chose.

#### Resources
- **24 apple tokens** — Red acrylic tokens. One of the two resource types you'll collect and deliver.
- **25 gem tokens** — Blue acrylic tokens. The other resource type.
- **12 quest tokens** — These get placed on the board when you Seek. If your seeker lands on one, you trigger a quest.

#### Cards & Dice
- **12 quest cards** — Each presents you with two options that have different friendship consequences (generous vs. greedy).
- **30 magic spell cards** — One-time-use abilities you can play for various effects. You can also attach them to pie fights for extra strength (this is important — they add 2-5 to your combat total).
- **8 personality cards** — Secret cards dealt at setup. Each one modifies a specific trophy requirement, making it easier for you to earn.
- **5 custom dice** — Red dice generate apples, blue dice generate gems, gold dice generate quest tokens. Each face shows a region on the board where you'll place the resource.
- **6 player mats** — These track your action choices and your power-up slots.
- **2 pie fight dials** — Secret dials for setting your pie bid during combat. You and your opponent each set one privately, then reveal simultaneously.

#### Solo Mode
- **1 Automountie board + tokens** — An AI opponent for solo play.

#### Other
- **1 setup tile** — Determines where starting resources get placed on the board.
- **1 painting guide + achievement sheet**
- **2 rulebooks** (multiplayer + solo)

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Setup
subtopics.content:
#### How the Game Works
My Little Scythe is a race to earn **4 trophies**. There are 8 different trophy categories, and you only need any 4 of them to trigger the endgame. On each turn, you pick one of three actions — Move, Seek, or Make — but here's the catch: you **cannot repeat the same action** you took last turn. You'll explore the Kingdom of Pomme, collect apples and gems, deliver them to Castle Everfree, complete quests, get into pie fights, and try to keep your friendship up (because if it drops too low, you're locked out of earning trophies entirely).

#### Setting Up the Board
1. **Unfold the game board** and place it in the center of the table.
2. **Pick your animal kingdom.** Grab the 2 matching seeker miniatures, 1 base camp tile, 1 action token, 4 trophy tokens, and 1 player mat.
3. **Place your base camp tile** on the board in the position shown for your player count.
4. **Put both of your seekers** on your base camp — they always start together.
5. **Set your friendship token** on **level 3** of the Friendship Track.
6. **Set your pie token** on **level 3** of the Pie Track.
7. **Shuffle the quest cards** and place them face-down near the board.
8. **Shuffle the spell cards**, deal **1 spell** to each player (keep it secret), and place the rest face-down as a draw pile.
9. **Separate the power-up tiles** into Move and Make piles, then shuffle each pile face-down.
10. **Place the setup tile** on Castle Everfree, then flip it — this tells you where to put the starting resources on the board.
11. **Deal 1 personality card** to each player (keep it secret — you'll choose when to reveal it later).
12. **Pick a first player** randomly. Play proceeds clockwise from there.

#### Checkpoint
Each player should now have: 2 seekers on their base camp, friendship at 3, pies at 3, 1 spell card in hand, 1 secret personality card, 4 trophy tokens ready to place, a player mat, and an action token. The board should have resources scattered on it from the setup tile.

subtopics.title: Turn Flow
subtopics.content:
#### Your Turn
On your turn, place your action token on **one** section of your player mat: **Move**, **Seek**, or **Make**. Here's the key restriction — you **cannot choose the same action** you took on your previous turn. (So if you Moved last turn, you must Seek or Make this turn.) Resolve the action, then check whether you've earned a trophy (you can claim **at most 1 trophy per turn**). Then play passes clockwise.

#### Action 1: Move
When you choose Move, you move **both** of your seekers, each one independently:
- **Without resources:** Your seeker can move up to **2 spaces** across the hex grid.
- **Carrying resources (apples or gems):** Your seeker can move only **1 space**, but it brings the resources along.

**Portals:** Those pink-bordered spaces on the board? They're portals — they count as adjacent to each other. Moving between portals costs just 1 movement, which makes them great shortcuts.

**Picking up resources:** When your seeker enters a space that has resources on it, it automatically picks them up and carries them. Just remember — once your seeker is carrying resources, its movement drops to 1 space.

**Delivering to Castle Everfree:** When your seeker carries resources into Castle Everfree, all carried resources are deposited there. If you deliver **4 apples in a single turn**, you earn the Apple Delivery trophy. Deliver **4 gems in a single turn** for the Gem Delivery trophy. (Both of your seekers can contribute to that total in the same turn.) After delivery, your seekers return to your base camp and you get **rejuvenated** — gain 1 spell OR 2 pies, and your action token is removed from your mat (which means you can freely choose any action next turn).

**Entering an occupied space:** If your seeker moves onto a space where an opponent's seeker is standing, a **pie fight** triggers immediately (more on that below).

After all your movement is done, resolve any **quests** on spaces where your seekers ended up.

#### Action 2: Seek
Seeking is how resources enter the game. Roll the **dice** shown on your player mat's Seek section. Each die result shows a **region** on the board:
- **Red dice** → Place 1 apple token from the supply on any space in the indicated region.
- **Blue dice** → Place 1 gem token on any space in the indicated region.
- **Gold dice** → Place 1 quest token on any **unoccupied** space in the indicated region.

You choose the exact space within each region. Here's a nice bonus: **placing a resource on a space occupied by an opponent's seeker** grants you **+1 friendship** per resource placed there. (This is free friendship — take advantage of it.)

#### Action 3: Make (Choose One)
The Make action lets you spend resources your seekers are carrying (or sitting on) to build up your capabilities. Pick one of these three options:
- **Bake Pies** — Remove **2 apples** from your seekers' spaces → gain **2 pies** on the Pie Track (max 10).
- **Conjure Spell** — Remove **2 gems** → draw **1 magic spell card** from the deck (no hand limit).
- **Power Up** — Remove **1 apple + 1 gem** → draw the top 3 tiles from either the Move or Make power-up pile. Keep 1, return the other 2 to the bottom. Place the chosen tile on your player mat — it **permanently upgrades** that action for the rest of the game.

subtopics.title: Pie Fights, Quests & Friendship
subtopics.content:
#### Pie Fights (Combat)
A pie fight happens whenever your seeker moves onto a space occupied by an opponent's seeker. Here's how it plays out, step by step:

1. **You (the attacker) lose 1 friendship immediately.** (This is the cost of aggression — it happens before anything else.)
2. Both players **secretly set** their pie fight dial to a number of pies (anywhere from 0 to your current pie total). For each seeker you have in the fight, you may also attach **1 magic spell card** to add its value (2-5) to your total.
3. Both players **reveal their dials simultaneously.**
4. **Highest total wins. If it's a tie, the ATTACKER wins.** (This is important — ties always favor the aggressor.)
5. All bid pies are **spent** (reduce your Pie Track accordingly). Any spell cards used are discarded.
6. The **loser's seekers** retreat back to their base camp, dropping all carried resources on the battle space.
7. The **winner** earns the **Pie Fight Victory trophy** (if they haven't already earned it).
8. The **loser** gets **rejuvenated** as consolation — gain 1 spell OR 2 pies, and their action token is removed from their mat (freeing up their action choice next turn).

**A few things to keep in mind:**
- You CAN fight with 0 pies (risky, but sometimes worth it if your opponent also has few pies).
- The Pie Fight Victory trophy is the **only trophy** you can earn on another player's turn (specifically, when you're defending and win).
- Spell cards add 2-5 to your total, making them powerful combat insurance. Holding spells makes opponents think twice about attacking you.

#### Quests
Quest tokens appear on the board when someone rolls gold dice during a Seek action. After you finish your Move action (including any pie fights that triggered), check whether either of your seekers is on a space with a quest token:
1. Remove the quest token from the board.
2. Draw 1 quest card.
3. You have three choices: **Option 1** (usually the generous path — a smaller reward but you gain friendship), **Option 2** (usually the greedy path — a bigger reward but you lose friendship), or **"No Thanks"** (return the card to the bottom of the deck). **Important:** choosing "No Thanks" does NOT count toward your Quest trophy.

Completing **2 quests** (by choosing Option 1 or 2 on two separate quest cards) earns you the **Quest trophy**.

#### The Friendship Track
Your friendship ranges from **0 to 8**, and everyone starts at **3**. Think of it as the game's moral compass — it rewards you for being generous and punishes you for being aggressive.

**Your friendship goes up when you:**
- Place resources on an opponent's seeker's space during Seek (+1 per resource)
- Choose the generous option on quest cards
- Use certain spell effects

**Your friendship goes down when you:**
- Initiate a pie fight (-1 immediately)
- Choose the greedy option on quest cards

**CRITICAL RULE — the friendship lock-out:** If your friendship drops **below 3**, you are **completely locked out of earning ANY trophies** until you restore it to 3 or higher. This is the game's primary anti-aggression mechanism, and it's the single most important thing for new players to understand. Don't pick fights until you've built a friendship cushion above 3.

Reaching friendship **level 8** earns you the **Friendship trophy**.

subtopics.title: Trophies & Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### The 8 Trophy Categories
You need **4 trophies** to trigger the endgame. There are 8 categories, and each can be earned once per player. On any given turn, you can claim **at most 1 trophy** (except during the Grand Finale — more on that below).

Here are all 8:
1. **Friendship Trophy** — Reach level **8** on the Friendship Track.
2. **Pie Trophy** — Reach level **8** on the Pie Track.
3. **Power Ups Trophy** — Have **both** a Move power-up tile AND a Make power-up tile on your player mat at the same time.
4. **Spell Trophy** — Hold **3 or more** magic spell cards in your hand at once.
5. **Apple Delivery Trophy** — Deliver exactly **4 apples** to Castle Everfree in a **single turn** (both your seekers can contribute to the total).
6. **Gem Delivery Trophy** — Deliver exactly **4 gems** to Castle Everfree in a **single turn** (both seekers can contribute).
7. **Quest Trophy** — Complete **2 quest cards** by choosing Option 1 or Option 2 (remember, "No Thanks" doesn't count).
8. **Pie Fight Victory Trophy** — **Win** a pie fight, either as attacker or defender. This is the only trophy you can earn on someone else's turn.

**Trophies are permanent.** Once you earn one, it stays even if you no longer meet the condition. (So if you hit Pie level 8 to earn the Pie trophy and then spend pies down to 4, the trophy is still yours. Same for Friendship, Spells, and all the rest.)

**Friendship lock-out reminder:** If your friendship is sitting **below 3**, you cannot earn any trophies at all until you get it back to 3 or higher.

#### Personality Cards
Remember that secret personality card you got during setup? It modifies one specific trophy's requirement, making it easier for you to earn. You can reveal it at any point during the game — the key is **timing your reveal** for maximum advantage (reveal it the moment you can claim a trophy that others didn't expect).

#### Endgame (Grand Finale)
The moment any player places their **4th trophy**, the Grand Finale begins. Finish the current round so that every player gets an equal number of turns. During the Grand Finale, the 1-trophy-per-turn limit is lifted — so if you can claim 2 trophies on your final turn, go for it.

#### Winning
1. The player with the **most trophies** wins.
2. Tied on trophies? **Higher friendship** breaks the tie.
3. Still tied? The player with **more pies** remaining wins.
4. Still tied? You share the victory.

#### Solo Mode (Automountie)
The Automountie is an AI opponent controlled by a set of action tokens and a decision flowchart. It earns trophies using the same rules you do — your goal is to reach 4 trophies before it does.

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Opening Moves (Turns 1-3)
Your first three turns should follow a natural rhythm:
- **Turn 1: Seek.** Roll your dice and place resources on the board near your seekers for easy pickup. Here's the trick — place at least a couple on spaces where opponents' seekers are sitting. You get +1 friendship per resource placed there, which is essentially free friendship while you seed the board with goodies.
- **Turn 2: Move.** Now send your seekers out to collect the resources you just placed. Keep in mind that carrying resources limits movement to 1 space, so you want those resources close to your starting position.
- **Turn 3: Make.** Your seekers should be carrying resources now. Spend 1 apple + 1 gem to Power Up — you'll draw 3 tiles from either the Move or Make pile, keep the best one, and place it on your mat. Getting your first power-up early pays dividends for the rest of the game.

#### The Fastest Path to 4 Trophies
When you're learning, aim for these four trophies — they're the most consistent combination:
1. **Power Ups** — Get both a Move AND a Make power-up (costs 1 apple + 1 gem each, across 2 separate Make actions). This is the most reliable trophy because it depends entirely on your own actions.
2. **Friendship** — Reach level 8. You start at 3 and gain +1 each time you place resources on opponents' spaces during Seek. Generous quest choices help too. Avoid pie fights in the early game to keep this climbing.
3. **Spells** — Hold 3 spell cards in your hand simultaneously. Each Conjure Spell action costs 2 gems and draws you 1 card. Three Make actions gets you there.
4. **Quest** — Complete 2 quests by moving your seekers to quest token spaces and choosing Option 1 or 2 (not "No Thanks").

#### Key Rule: Don't Drop Below Friendship 3
This is the single most important thing for new players to internalize. If your friendship drops below 3, you are **completely locked out of earning trophies** — any of them, not just the Friendship trophy. Every pie fight costs 1 friendship. Since you start at 3, a single unprepared fight drops you to 2, and suddenly you're stuck earning nothing until you climb back up. Build your friendship above 3 before you even think about combat.

#### When to Fight
Combat earns you a trophy (Pie Fight Victory) but costs 1 friendship. Here's when the timing is right:
- **Your friendship is at 5 or higher** — you can absorb the -1 without any danger of lock-out.
- **You have spell cards** to attach to your dial (each adds 2-5 to your total — that's a massive swing).
- **Your opponent is carrying resources** — if you win, they drop everything on the battle space for you to grab.
- **You're the attacker** — ties go to the attacker, so even a close matchup favors you when you're the one initiating.

#### Seek Friendship Strategy
Placing resources on opponents' spaces during Seek gives +1 friendship per resource. This is **free friendship** — you're populating the board with resources (which you need to do anyway) AND gaining friendship simultaneously. Try to target occupied spaces whenever possible; gaining 2-3 friendship per Seek action is realistic and keeps you well above the lock-out threshold.

#### Action Restriction
You cannot repeat the same action twice in a row, so plan your turns in natural pairs: Seek then Move (collect what you just placed), Move then Make (spend what you just collected), Make then Seek (refill the board). This Seek-Move-Make cycle is the backbone of your game plan.

#### Common Mistakes
- **Fighting at friendship 3.** This is the number one beginner trap. One fight drops you to 2 and locks you out of all trophies. Build friendship first, fight later.
- **Forgetting the action restriction.** You can't Seek twice in a row. Always be thinking one turn ahead about which actions pair together.
- **Ignoring spells in combat.** Spell cards add 2-5 to your pie fight total. Holding 3 spells earns you the Spell trophy AND makes you a serious combat threat — opponents will avoid picking fights with you.
- **Delivering fewer than 4 resources.** You need exactly 4 apples (or 4 gems) delivered to Castle Everfree in a single turn for the delivery trophy. Delivering 3 gets you nothing. Use both seekers carrying resources to hit that 4-resource threshold.
- **Revealing your personality card too early.** Your personality card is a surprise — it lets you claim a trophy under modified conditions that opponents aren't expecting. Wait until the optimal moment to reveal it; the element of surprise is half its value.

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of My Little Scythe

**After Setup**: Take a moment to check that everyone is ready. Each player should have 2 seekers sitting on their base camp, friendship at 3, pies at 3, 1 spell card in hand, 1 secret personality card, and 4 trophy tokens waiting to be placed. Resources from the setup tile should be scattered across the board.

**Turn 1 — Seek**: You're going to start by rolling your dice. Let's say you roll 2 red (apples) and 2 blue (gems). Place those apples and gems on the board — put some near your seekers so you can grab them easily next turn. Now here's a smart move: place at least 1 resource on a space where an opponent's seeker is sitting. That gives you +1 friendship, bumping you to 4. Free friendship on turn 1!

**Turn 2 — Move** (you can't repeat Seek): Now move both your seekers toward the resources you just placed. Without carrying anything, each seeker can move up to 2 spaces. As you enter spaces with apples and gems, your seekers pick them up automatically. Just remember — once a seeker is carrying resources, it can only move 1 space.

**Turn 3 — Make** (you can't repeat Move): Your seekers are loaded with resources. Spend 1 apple + 1 gem to choose Power Up — you'll draw 3 tiles from the Move pile, keep the one you like best, and put the other 2 back on the bottom. Place your new power-up on your player mat. One more power-up tile (from the other pile) and you'll earn the **Power Ups trophy**.

**Turn 4 — Seek** (you can't repeat Make): Roll your dice again. Place resources near your seekers and on opponents' spaces for more friendship. If a gold die shows up, place a quest token on an unoccupied space in that region — you'll want to land on it later.

**Turn 5 — Move**: Collect resources again. If there's a quest token along your path, land on it — you'll draw a quest card and choose one of its options. That counts as quest 1 of 2 toward the **Quest trophy**.

**Turn 6 — Make**: Spend 1 apple + 1 gem for a second Power Up, this time from the Make pile. You now have both a Move and a Make power-up on your mat — that earns you the **Power Ups trophy**! Place your first trophy token on the Trophy Track. That's 1 of 4.

**Mid-Game**: Keep riding the Seek-Move-Make cycle. Your goals from here: build friendship toward 8 (that's the Friendship trophy), accumulate 3 spell cards in hand (Spell trophy), complete a second quest (Quest trophy), and watch for chances to deliver 4 apples or 4 gems to Castle Everfree in one turn if the board sets up for it.

**The Home Stretch**: Once you have 3 trophies, you're one away from triggering the endgame. This is when you should consider revealing your personality card — if it makes a trophy easier to claim, now is the time. When you place your 4th trophy, the Grand Finale triggers. Finish out the current round so everyone gets equal turns, and then the player with the most trophies wins.

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Trophy Rush vs. Slow Build
You need 4 trophies to win, but there are two fundamentally different ways to get there. The **rush strategy** targets the easiest trophies — Power Ups, a Pie Fight Victory, a couple of quests — and races to 4 before opponents can set up their engines. The **slow build** takes time to accumulate resources, upgrade both actions with power-up tiles, and then claims 3-4 trophies in rapid succession over consecutive turns. Against experienced players, the slow build is risky because someone else may Grand Finale before you're ready.

#### Friendship Track Mastery
The Friendship Track does more than just threaten lock-out below 3. Reaching level 8 earns you a trophy, and staying high on the track keeps all your other trophy paths open. The best way to climb: **Seek generously**. Every time you place a resource on an opponent's seeker space, you get +1 friendship for free. Combine that with choosing generous quest options (Option 1), and you can realistically hit 8 by mid-game without ever slowing down your resource engine.

#### Delivery Trophy Planning
The delivery trophies (4 apples or 4 gems to Castle Everfree in a single turn) require careful setup. You need **both seekers carrying resources** and converging on the castle in the same Move action. Place resources in a line between your seekers and the castle during Seek, collect them on one Move, then deliver on the next. Power-up tiles that extend your movement range make this much more consistent — prioritize Move power-ups if you're going for a delivery trophy.

#### Combat as a Trophy Tool
Pie fights cost 1 friendship but can earn you the Pie Fight Victory trophy — and the loser drops all their carried resources on the battle space. The ideal attack: wait until your friendship is at **5 or higher**, you're holding **1-2 spell cards** for dial boosting (each adds 2-5 to your total), and your target is **carrying valuable resources** you can scoop up. Remember that **ties go to the attacker**, so even matching your opponent's investment wins you the fight. One advanced trick: if you're low on pies but holding strong spells, you can bid 0 pies and rely entirely on spell value to win.

#### Power-Up Tile Priorities
Not all power-up tiles are equal. **Move power-ups** that grant extra movement or let you carry resources without the speed penalty are game-changing — they make delivery trophies far easier and let you reach quest tokens faster. **Make power-ups** that reduce costs or add bonus effects to Bake Pies / Conjure Spell let your resources stretch further. Prioritize whichever power-up type aligns with your trophy plan, and remember: having one of each type earns the Power Ups trophy automatically.

#### Personality Card Timing
Your personality card is your hidden ace. It modifies one trophy's requirement to make it easier — but only after you reveal it. The optimal reveal is the moment you can **immediately claim a trophy** that opponents weren't expecting. Revealing early gives opponents time to adjust; revealing at the perfect moment can catch the table off guard and push you to 4 trophies before anyone can respond.
